heading: Settlement of claims for damages caused by the Postal Service
label: 2603 Settlement of claims for damages caused by the Postal Service
text: When the Postal Service finds a claim for damage to persons or property resulting from the operation of the Postal Service to be a proper charge against the United States, and it is not cognizable under , it may adjust and settle the claim.section 2672 of title 28
notes: Effective DateSection effective , pursuant to Resolution No. 71–9 of the Board of Governors. See , set out as a note preceding .July 1, 1971section 15(a) of Pub. L. 91–375section 101 of this title

Charge Against Postal Revenues for Settlement of Claims for Damages and for Losses Resulting From Unavoidable Casualty, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 89–57, title II, § 20179 Stat. 200June 30, 1965“That hereafter settlement of claims, pursuant to law, current and prior fiscal years, for damages, and for losses resulting from unavoidable casualty shall be paid from postal revenues.”
amendments: Charge Against Postal Revenues for Settlement of Claims for Damages and for Losses Resulting From Unavoidable Casualty, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 89–57, title II, § 20179 Stat. 200June 30, 1965“That hereafter settlement of claims, pursuant to law, current and prior fiscal years, for damages, and for losses resulting from unavoidable casualty shall be paid from postal revenues.”
